examples.english: Did you see the children for me?.

gloss: to go with, go according to, depend on

examples.english: It went according to how much the money was.
This person has been bewitched hither.
May the sun kill you! (a curse).
Spread the maize out to dry!.

gloss: be done, finished; pass by (in time)
to egg on, goad, spur, foment, inflame, incite
to perish. ko-bek v.ven. /ko-pek/ to be finished (of a supply of s.t.) ko-bek-chi v.app. /ko-bek-ci/ to finish on

examples.english: The flour has been finished on food.
The cows are coming straight home (without dawdling on the way).
The people crossed paths (unbeknowst to one another).
May you become a fool! (a curse).
It's not difficult, it is not a big deal; anyone can do it.
The elderly lady's bones have become brittle.

gloss: to go by (i.e. one way rather than another)
to become pregnant before marriage; become pregnant (of dog, etc.)

examples.english: The girl is pregnant before marriage.
Kogochi siling' mut; kowekwo somok ak kole ko chutok kitio. Kibet swindled me out of my money. I gave him five shillings; he returned three to me, and he said that was all there was.
examples.phonetic: _ke:-purpu:r ki-pe:t ce:pkô:nt-ô:k-yu:k. Ka:-ka:-ci _siHng mu:t; kô:-we:k-wa somokakko-le ka: cu:t-ak kityo

examples.english: Burersei enk itok. What is Jepkeny doing? (She) is lazing in bed.

gloss: to end (work) at a certain point
to be lucky in s.t. which will result in happiness
to praise a person in terms of his oxen or his bravery
to compliment, make an uncalled for compliment
to spurt, squirt, gush, pour out
to whisper, gossip, slander, criticise, backbite

examples.english: Fve slipped on account of the chicken droppings.

gloss: to slip away accidentally(e.g. soapfrom hand)
to suffer the consequences ofone's deed; to be in despair

examples.english: It's bad for the eyes to bulge on account of alcohol.

gloss: [< ke-chil to press] 1. spear butt. 2. wedge
to search for an entrance or exit (e.g. of water moving slowly looking for aroute)

examples.english: The water seeped into the house.

gloss: [cf. ke-cirjir to rub feet in mud] to make a laughing stock of (lit. rub teeth), make a mockery of

examples.english: The children have made a laughing stock of this person.

gloss: to be scraped, skinned off by s.t. dull
to hold back or struggle when pulled
to hold back from, resist doing
to passby one another, to miss on the way (one going, the other coming)

examples.english: (S/he) and Tapketlal passed by one another.
Make the children step it up!.

gloss: to rush about to get done on time. 2. bustle about
to plaster a house for the third and last time with smoothmud, to apply finishc ing coats of plaster to a house
to prepare, fix, mend, repair, arrange
to pour out the last bit
to make noise sucking the last bit ofliquid from a container with a straw

examples.english: The elder slurped (from) the beer pot.
(S/he) is saying those words in jest.
(She) is lowering the water (from her back).

gloss: to come down, lower oneself down

examples.english: The cat is coming down from that tree.
People are starving in this country.

gloss: to become runny (as a result of overcooking)
to retreat. 2. to commit strongly to doing s.t. then later reduce the strength of this

examples.nandi: Kole Tapketnot nyotoretin; oitio kochuchuch kei.
examples.english: Tapketnot said going to help you; then she went back on it.

gloss: to go backward, draw backward surrepitiously

examples.english: The sheep/goat drew backwards in order to eat the millet.
That person has a stomach ache; food goes right through (him/her).

gloss: to be still in sight or within reach while going away, descend
to go under horizon (sun, person walking in distance)

examples.english: May a wind carry you under the horizon with it (a curse).
Don't let Mr. Kogo slip away.

gloss: to pass by one another without seeing, miss one another (e.g. one arriving when the other has just left)

examples.english: Rinse the clothes until the suds are gone.

gloss: to hurry, hasten, rush, quicken this way
to c hurry, hasten, rush that way

examples.english: The child is stuck on top of the tree.

gloss: to behave badly, misbehave, mistreat someone
to drink (all liquids except milk, see ke-lu)

examples.english: One doesn't skin a calf before its mother's eyes (don't be cruel).
The man of that house has died.

gloss: to be a great nuisance, menace

examples.english: Rats have been a big nuisance in thefield.

gloss: to be blessed with children, have children
to take on, wdth, by (means of)

examples.english: The child took the food on the plate.

gloss: to pass time talking to people for no particular reason, amuse oneself, dilly-dally and chat with whoever is around

examples.english: (S/he) has struggled with it alone (bothered herself with).
He's the one who is coming.

gloss: ochre (for hair and body in ceremonies)

examples.english: Mrs. Chemartim applied ochre to (her) head.

gloss: to divert water (to drain or irrigate)
to fling or spray (e.g. water or millet seed), sow
to remove oneselffrom, avoid, stay away from

examples.english: Stay away from the people of that house.

gloss: to make oneself respectable, make oneself decent

examples.english: Cover yourself! Be respectable! Make yourself decent!.
The lizard peeped out ofthe cave.

gloss: to be volatile, impulsive, confused, scatter-brained

examples.english: Take this torch so that you shine the way with (it).

gloss: to sleep fitfully, waking up frequently
to surround and close in on
to mislead, deceive (especially by promising s.t. which will not materialise)

examples.english: One doesn't lead someone on, make them have high hopes (lit. lie to the stomach).

gloss: to flirt with, play up to; be kittenish, playful; be mischievous

examples.english: (S/he) is being playful with the child.
Kiprono is looking badly at Kibet.

gloss: to replace, to occupy before another

examples.english: Koitar. Replace my soap! You have finished it.

gloss: to soften beans or dry meat with sodawater (munyuk), tenderise

examples.english: (S/he) is carrying the child on her back.

gloss: to stop an unwanted guest from entering a beerparty

examples.english: Stop that person from entering the party!.

gloss: to drive hither (can include cattle, sheep/goats)
to take s.t. back, drive thither

examples.english: Take the cows back (to where they belong).

gloss: to go back on one's, repeat one's

examples.english: One doesn't go back on (or repeat) one's word.

gloss: to dilute, 2. to counterbalance, nullify, neutralise

examples.english: (S/he) diluted the milk (to alleviate the sourness).

gloss: to spray hither (carried by the wind)

examples.english: (S/he) has loaded the house with rubbish.

gloss: to postpone, put off, procrastinate; stretch out
to come to a dead end

examples.english: The road has come to a dead end.

gloss: to come crashing down on s.o., to be at an impasse for s.o., be stuck with no place to go

examples.english: The world came crashing down on her/him, s/he was at an impasse.
Kibusio hit me on the head!.

gloss: [cf. kigochi to give to] 1. to give (towards speaker)

examples.english: Kono! Give it here! Give it to me!.

gloss: to put away, store, save for the future
to suck, draw up. 2. to smoke (e.g. pipe)

examples.english: The cattle ate the millet while the shepherd was 'out to lunch'.
The cat is arching its back.

gloss: to pick (at) something small: burrs (kipkolet), residue on bottom of pan after cooking porridge or milk (kipkuniet), nits)

examples.english: (S/he) is picking the porridge stuck to the bottom of the saucepan.

examples.nandi: Kigurguri kitio; kemut lakwet ra kogeny?
examples.english: (It) is astonishing; (s/he) brought the child again today?.
(S/he) didn't make a dent in the work.
You peeled the bark of the kerundut shrub.

gloss: to walk carefully to avoid stepping on mud or kicking dust. 2. measure by pacing, pace out/off
to knead (polenta with a wooden spoon), cook (kimiet)

examples.english: The lady ofthis house is cooking.
The old man farted while he was sleeping.

gloss: to sort out, to choose one by one. 2. to pick up from the floor

examples.english: (S/he) is picking up grain from the floor.
The child was put in the middle.

gloss: to wonder, be surprised, vexed, baffled, startled, mystified, stupified

examples.english: The drunk fell upside down into that river.

gloss: [cf ke-gon to give hither] 1. to give to, dedicate, grant

examples.english: Five cows were given for bridewealth.
The Molel and Boit families can intermarry.
(S/he) fell and almost broke (his/her) spinal cord.

examples.nandi: Kegos teta ra; alen kobiyo omut.
examples.english: The cow lactated today; I think it ate its fill yesterday.
One doesn't put on a garment with a caterpillar in it (see chebutietcaterpillar).

gloss: to rise from water, to come across water, to come up from the bottom of a valley or hole
bring hither across a river or road (opposite of ki-landa)

examples.english: (S/he) is bringing the children across the river.

gloss: to go across water to a place

examples.nandi: Kogilanda chebioset kwo kot ab boisio.
examples.english: The woman has been carried across to the men's house, i.e. is exposed to men's rituals to change herbad luck.

gloss: to look beautiful in striped clothing

examples.english: (S/he) painted the house until it was striped.

gloss: not to remember a former acquaintance, mistake one thing for another, err, hurt by mistake
to have enough space, be ample, be loosefitting, spacious, wide
to climb a steep hill at an angle
[cf ke-la to braid] to spin (hair, thread), braid by spinning

examples.english: (His) hair has been spun (two strands rolledtogether)back.

gloss: to take from place to place trying to fmd the right location

examples.english: The prisoner was taken (from one gaol to another).
What do I do with it?.
Jepkoskei is paying for it today.

gloss: to die of old age, die a natural death

examples.english: Grandfather has died of old age.

gloss: to graze cows in morning before they have been milked

examples.english: Who is grazing the cattle early today?.
The person has vanished at this river.

gloss: to take in a certain direction

examples.english: In whatdirection did the babysitter take the child?.
The hare is running rapidly heading that way.
I've turned the house inside out looking for it.
(S/he) pestered the child until it cried.

gloss: to empty, pour out all there is

examples.english: The vegetables have spoiled; throw them away!.

examples.nandi: Kalumda kei chicho eng' yo koimen.
examples.english: That person is standing like a post over there all day long.
I think he has become lost on the way.

gloss: to disappear, vanish; lose one's way
to let blood solidify, coagulate, set
to ambush, ko-lobul-do v.itv. /ko-lapul-ta/ to dent
to conduct oneself like a neurotic person
to strew this way and that

examples.english: Things were strewn all about in thehouse.

gloss: to hit (less strongly than ke-kwer), spank, swipe, smack, strike

examples.english: (S/he) diverted the money of another person.

gloss: to be arrogant, conceited, snobbish, swellheaded

examples.english: It's offensive to persist begging because of a cup.

gloss: to complain in a non-stop fashion

examples.english: (S/he) scattered the things in the house.
The country has become sour today.

gloss: to contaminate when one is impure, to cast contaminating shadow on

examples.english: It has become a little dark outside.

gloss: to suck and chew (as with sugar cane)
to massage a pregnant woman gently with oil in order to put the baby back in the proper position

examples.english: The old man has become drunk until he's in a daze.
Put the child gently to bed.

gloss: to scrutinise and be dissatisfied with because of the amount

examples.english: She has refused the food (because it was little).
Are you able to carry the child?.

gloss: to brave, endure, put up with (s.t. of short duration)

examples.english: These things are what I can't put up with.

gloss: to go down with the sun, to go down in defeat

examples.english: May you go down with the sun!.

gloss: to put across or crossways. ko-mur-to v.itv. /ko-mur-ta/ 1. to turn off

examples.nandi: Omurotoni ra ingot ko mebuch kot.
examples.english: I will circumcise you today if you don't clean the house.

gloss: [cf ke-mus to come early] to go early in the morning
not to be able to distinguish or discern
to cut in two with one blow; hit hard with a heavy cane. 2. to poison

examples.english: (S/he) indicated that this person was good.

gloss: to announce, tell thither. 2. to tell on

examples.english: Don't tell on me, tell on Jepchirjir!.

gloss: to emerge, come out of Mong'u iyeto

examples.nandi: The smoke is coming out. /mang-u iyye-ta/ Che komong' chu.
examples.english: This is the result, result (lit. what came out (is) this).
examples.phonetic: ce kô:-mang cu, cu ce ka-mang

examples.english: Please present me with some milk.

examples.nandi: Megochini kimoik sesenik eng' ko si konaita.
examples.english: Don't give the dogs food in the house so that they become accustomed to (it).

gloss: to agree, get along, be in love

examples.english: They get along, are in love.

gloss: to hold back, delay. 2. to hold with, delay with

examples.english: Hold the pot with a cloth so it doesn't bum you.
He is holding on to that money of his.

gloss: to be near to s.t., closeto s.t

examples.english: Negite yo. (It's) near here. (It's) near there.

gloss: to go on and on with

examples.english: (S/he) goes on and on with a litany.

gloss: to outstrip, surpass in a physical competition

examples.english: Konenet Jepkogei Jepkoech. Jepkoech and Jepkogei wrestled yesterday and Jepkoech outstripped Jepkogei.

gloss: to rain in a steady, nagging fashion; (fig.) to harp

examples.english: It's drizzling like the irokwet tree.

gloss: to blurt out secret about, be a tell-tale, betray, implicate

examples.english: The sun has cracked the earth.

gloss: to have a rough surface, be chapped, be cracked

examples.english: The house has cracked, i.e. the plaster has split.
The sun has cracked the house (hither).
The kiptang'ing'iyet fly is buzzing around the ear.
Tilt it to face this way.
(S/he) put too much (sugar) in the tea.
Give me a little bit of milk to smell the polenta with.

gloss: to wrestle with tough meat while trying to chew

examples.english: The thigh muscle is bulging (i.e. is tough).
Who does this saliva belong to?.

gloss: to give a slobbery kiss to

examples.english: (She) wasgiven a slobbery kiss (by a drunk, by an infant).

gloss: to creep (because of weakness), drag oneself along

examples.english: (S/he) is complaining incessantly, /ingunynguny-i/ ng'uran ke-ng'uran v.
The cow is entwining itselfs around the tree (i.e. with its rope).

gloss: to attract attention of furtively, beckon to come (by hand, wink or other slight movement)
to soak in rain, be wet
to soak (in order to clean), lubricate
to take without checking if it belongs to another, misappropriate
to be in trouble, in need
to be languid, languish; be weary and sleepy

examples.english: The country is watery, is a bog.

gloss: to multiply, do again, repeat over

examples.english: How many times did (s/he) call you?.
The words which Kiptaigor is saying turn my stomach.

gloss: to do before another, be ahead of, be first; catch off guard

examples.english: (S/he) was first, (s/he) caught (her/him) off guard; I was first, I caught (him/her) off guard.

gloss: to be ahead hither, catch off guard hither, get for first

examples.english: I found mother in the attic.
I found mother in the attic.
(S/he) is as stubbom as a kongoni.

gloss: to look good, be becoming, be suitable
to tear or dart this way

examples.english: Let it rain so the grass can sprout.

gloss: to serve, share or divide thither, distribute
to pat with palm of hand. 2. to set foot

examples.english: No one can set footon(his/her) door.
The cups are rattling in the attic.

gloss: to stand around looking lost, to be useless, to be 'out of it'

examples.english: The bull made this cow pregnant.

gloss: to dash up to and grab, snatch, push to force

examples.english: (S/he) thrust the forest, i.e. went through pathless forest.

gloss: to top up beer in pot

examples.english: Give me the pot to top up the beer.

gloss: to follow in order, be next in order
to follow in order, be next in order

examples.english: Round up the sheep and goats!.

gloss: to wound, kill, murder another Nandi
to purr (cat, boiling vegetables under covered pot, car in distance)
to pile up, heap up, gather together in one place
to bump into, collide with, crash into

examples.english: (S/he) crashed the car into the tree until it was dented.

gloss: to go on visit, to vacation
to sleep during the day habitually because oflaziness
gather together and scoop up a bunch of things. 2. to tackle, take on s.t. difficult to pick up
sound or tune made by water at the bottom of a waterfall, jingling of thigh-bells
to do in a slipshod fashion

examples.english: (S/he) did the field in a slipshod fashion.
Kogateny oinet. The cattle have difficulty. The river has dried up.

gloss: sticking out (usually of cow's horns, metaphorically ofteeth)

examples.english: The teeth are large with spaces between them.
Wei, kwosikte. Did you succeed? Yes, I succeeded.
Kogosiele artet kotutiet kutit. Maggots have swarmed over the sheep's tail (tails of female sheep are cut short so they may conceive).

gloss: not to agree with one another about

examples.english: Those people didn't come to an agreement with one another about the matter.

gloss: to assault, insult. 2. to concentrate on

examples.english: (S/he) is biting and blowing (playing up and then playing down, dramatising s.t. and then making lightofit).

gloss: to be reduced (in swelling). 2. to cower
to beg, ask for, ask permission

examples.english: God, I have prayed to you.

gloss: to become tangy and smooth in consistency (of milk made into mursik)
to bring back, recoup, save, rescue

examples.english: Rescue the child so the dog doesn't eat it.
The cow has rejected the calf.

gloss: to be in a state of light trance, to nap

examples.nandi: Tandus ko bo tyony, ko ng'wan ko bo chi.
examples.english: What is pleasant for an animal is bitter for aperson (one's man's meat is another man's poison).
examples.phonetic: _ta:ntu:s ko pa tya:ny, ko _ngwa:n ko pa ci:

gloss: to be different from one another, to be separate

examples.english: The bird landed on that tree.

gloss: to step on the ground, to put the foot down on

examples.english: The child has dirtied the clothing (steppedontheclothing withdirt).

gloss: to walk in a lumbering way
to butt or ram each other
to put decorations around eyes (spots made of a white substance, e.g., ground millet mixed with liquid) on top of clay paint on face. 2. to be exposed to

examples.english: The food was exposed to flies.
Ingooro chito kotuitui. The dog is a coward. If it sees a person, it retreats.
May you be a sibling of a hyena! (a curse).

gloss: to scan, look at in the distance; keep an eye on

examples.english: What are you looking at over yonder?.

gloss: to look way beyond, to scan

examples.english: Jepkogei is outside gazing over yonder.

examples.nandi: Expressions: Give a name to a child!

examples.english: (S/he) took the food from the child.

gloss: to appear, be visible, be noticeable, come to life

examples.english: To smash a person's head so that the brains are splattered.
No one can wash the hand for a man (a man is never grateful).

gloss: to relinguish, release; allow to go
to return from, come back from
to be suitable for each other

examples.english: Kiptogom and Kipsalbei are incompatible with one another, unsuitable for each other.

gloss: to scatter (intr.), disperse (intr.), flee away from

examples.nandi: Koyogochi Cherono ingorienyi ko kosirtoe koinyi.
examples.english: I handed Cherono her dress when I passed by her house.
examples.phonetic: ka:-yaga:-ci ce:-ro:no inkor-ye:- _nyi: ko ka:-sir-ta-e: koi _nyi:

gloss: to wake up in the middle of the night

examples.english: Let's go to sleep; people are stirring.
Those children are the same age.
